name: Tenant Payment Setup
description: Tenant autopay configuration with payment method management, ACH/card setup, autopay toggle, and upcoming payment preview.

standard.description: Full payment setup view with autopay toggle, saved payment methods, and upcoming payment preview

slots.add-method: Add bank account or card form

visual_brief: Settings form for tenant autopay configuration. An autopay toggle switch at the top enables/disables automatic payments with a description of what it does. Below, a payment methods section shows saved cards/bank accounts as rows with masked account number, type icon (Visa, Mastercard, bank), and a star icon for the default method. 'Add Payment Method' button opens a card/ACH input form. An upcoming payments section shows the next payment amount, date, and property/unit in a summary card. If autopay is off, a manual 'Pay Now' button appears.

responsive.mobile: Full-width form. Payment method cards stack. Toggle switch prominent at top. Pay Now button full-width and sticky.
responsive.tablet: Standard form layout. Payment methods and upcoming payments visible together.
responsive.desktop: Comfortable form width. All sections visible. Payment methods in a clean list.

layout_hints.setup_priority: Lead with current payment readiness first: autopay state, active method, and next charge. Deeper method management should sit just below that primary understanding.
layout_hints.method_clarity: Masked account identity, default state, and next payment context should be tightly grouped so users trust what will be charged and when.
layout_hints.mobile_sequence: On small screens, keep autopay state, current method, and manual pay fallback in one simple vertical sequence rather than scattering them.

motion.micro: Autopay toggles and payment-method selection may confirm state changes with restrained visual feedback.
motion.transitions: Adding or switching methods should feel trustworthy and deliberate, especially around sensitive payment information.

keyboard: Autopay controls, payment methods, and upcoming payment actions should proceed in a clear top-to-bottom order.
announcements: Autopay state, default method, and next payment timing should be communicated semantically so the setup remains understandable without card or badge styling alone.

composition.MethodCard: Card(d-surface, active?: selected) > [CardBrandIcon + MaskedNumber(mono-data) + Expiry(text-muted) + DefaultBadge?(d-annotation)]
